workflow_name: Internal Account Review Transcript -> Team Actions
user_message: Just got this from Sarah Wilson at Litware Inc -- I need help figuring out consumption trend. The customer escalated this morning.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Litware Inc
Date: 2024-01-02
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on consumption trend from our last session.

[00:07:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:11:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:15:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on budget approval and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:18:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:22:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Let me add some context -- we've been working on timeline constraints and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:25:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on data residency and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:28:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

handoffs.condition: team coaching or risk review input

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF52-N trap (Separation of customer-facing draft from internal brief when one unified output ); produces complete Team action items with owners, coaching notes (constructive frame), customer-facing draft (sanitized
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF52-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF52-N failure mode (Separation of customer-facing draft from internal brief when one unified output )
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF52-N -- Separation of customer-facing draft from internal brief when one unified output
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
